Verifier explicitly checks this bound against the translated protein length. The actual longest ORF length is 430, which safely exceeds this minimum.

Python translation of the corrupted FASTA string embedded in setup_task.sh. The prompt asks to translate the longest ORF. Due to synthetic manipulations (accidental duplications in the sequence), the longest ORF is 430 amino acids long, contradicting the 370 maximum limit enforced by the verifier.

privileged_info_summary: The input GAPDH mRNA sequence is a synthetically corrupted version of NM_002046.7, containing duplicated regions at both the 5 prime end and internally. Consequently, the longest ORF starts at an inserted upstream ATGTGG and translates to 430 amino acids. Even if starting at the true GAPDH start codon, the translated protein is 374 amino acids long due to the internal duplication. Both lengths violate the verifier strict maximum limit of 370 amino acids. Thus, a perfectly executing agent will inevitably lose points for the protein length check, although they can still pass the task overall. Additionally, the sequence contains the Kozak motif ACCATGG but completely lacks the AATAAA polyadenylation signal due to 3 prime truncation.
